title: Battle of Tewkesbury 1471, Tewkesbury - 1000039
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Battle of Tewkesbury 1471, battlefield registration, Bloody Meadow/Gastons features, Severn Ham SSSI mention, and group value with Abbey Church of St Mary. Does not verify every Tewkesbury module subsite.

title: Abbey Church of St Mary, Tewkesbury - 1201159
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports the Abbey Church of St Mary / Tewkesbury Abbey as a listed heritage asset. Wider monastery chronology and all river-gate claims remain outside this source.

title: Hereford Cathedral - Home of the Mappa Mundi
notes: Directly supports Hereford Cathedral, Mappa Mundi, Chained Library, and preservation context. Does not verify all western marches places.

title: Dinedor Camp, Dinedor - 1001758
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Dinedor Camp as a scheduled promontory hillfort near the Wye/Lugg confluence. Does not verify all Herefordshire corridor sites.

title: Arthur's Stone, Dorstone, Herefordshire
notes: Directly supports Arthur's Stone as a Neolithic chambered tomb. The attached Arthur legend remains folklore and is not upgraded as factual history.

title: Woodchester Roman villa, Woodchester - 1004839
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Woodchester Roman Villa and the Orpheus Mosaic. Other Stroud/Frome radials remain unresolved by this source.

title: Uley Long Barrow (Hetty Pegler's Tump), Uley, Gloucestershire
notes: Directly supports Uley Long Barrow / Hetty Pegler's Tump as a Neolithic chambered long barrow. Does not verify every Stroud module site.

title: The Metalworking Evidence from West Hill, Uley, Gloucs.
notes: Directly supports West Hill, Uley as a Roman temple excavation context and metalworking evidence. Does not verify broader radial interpretation.

title: An Archaeological Survey in the Severn Vale, Gloucestershire: National Mapping Programme Highlight Report
notes: Supports Uley Bury as an Iron Age hillfort/settlement feature in the Severn Vale project. Other Stroud sites require separate records.

title: Corinium Museum - Discover the archaeology of the Cotswolds
notes: Supports Cirencester/Corinium, Roman town finds, Roman mosaics, and Corinium Dobunnorum as a fort/capital context. Does not verify all listed Corinium module subsites.

title: History of Cirencester Amphitheatre
notes: Directly supports Cirencester Amphitheatre, Corinium naming, early second-century construction, and Roman urban context. Other module places require further sources.

title: Chedworth Roman Villa, Gloucestershire
notes: Supports Chedworth Roman Villa as one of the largest second-century AD Roman villas in England with artefacts including mosaic fragments. Does not verify all Corinium module entries.

title: VCH Gloucestershire: Volume XVI, Bagendon
author_or_body: Victoria County History / Institute of Historical Research
notes: Supports Bagendon landscape, ancient routeways, brook/Churn context, and oolitic limestone geology. The oppidum claim still needs a direct archaeological source record.

title: Camp Hill promontory fort and Romano-British temple complex, Lydney - 1017373
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Lydney Park/Camp Hill, the Nodens temple complex, River Severn floodplain setting, Roman iron mining, and temple/baths/mansio structures. Does not verify Clearwell Caves, Puzzlewood, or all Forest of Dean entries.

title: Roman Villa at Clearwell Farm, Newland - 1406971
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports Roman occupation near Clearwell and group value within a key Roman iron-working area. Does not directly verify Clearwell Caves or Puzzlewood coin hoard.

title: Research Framework for the Forest of Dean District
notes: Supports Forest of Dean archaeological research framing, Lydney/Nodens, Roman temple discussion, and Roman-period material context. Specific cave/hoard claims remain partly unresolved.

title: The Cotswold Way
notes: Directly supports the Cotswold Way as a 102-mile National Trail from Chipping Campden to Bath along the western edge of the Cotswold Hills. Does not verify Belas Knap or detailed Jurassic geology.

title: Belas Knap Long Barrow
notes: Directly supports Belas Knap as a Gloucestershire long barrow on the Cotswold Way and links it with other Gloucestershire Neolithic long barrows. Detailed geology still requires a geological source.
